segments.text: The kingdom of heaven is like ten girls, who took their lamps with them to go and meet the bridegroom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Five were foolish, and five were wise.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The foolish girls took their lamps but didn't take any oil with them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: while the wise took jars of oil with them as well as their lamps.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The bridegroom took a long time and all the girls became drowsy and fell asleep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At midnight the shout came, ‘Look, the bridegroom's here! Come out and meet him!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All the girls got up and trimmed the wicks of their lamps. The foolish girls said to the wise ones,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.’ But the wise girls replied,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘No, because otherwise there won't be enough for both you and for us. Go to the shopkeepers and buy some oil for yourselves.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: While they went to buy oil, the bridegroom arrived, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding, and the door was locked shut.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The other girls came later. ‘Lord, Lord,’ they called, ‘Open the door for us!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But he answered, ‘I tell you the truth: I don't know you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So stay alert, for you don't know the day, or the hour.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It's like a man who went away on a trip. He called in his servants and entrusted them with what he owned.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: To one of them he gave five talents, [^158] to another he gave two, and to another one, according to their different abilities. Then he left.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately the one with five talents went and invested them in a business, and made another five talents.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way the one with two talents made another two.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the man who'd received the one talent went off and dug a hole and hid his master's money.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A long time later the master of those servants returned, and settled accounts with them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The one with five talents came and presented the other five talents. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘You gave me five talents. Look, I've made a profit of five talents.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His master said to him, ‘You've done well—you are a good, faithful servant. You have proved yourself trustworthy over small things, so now I'm placing you in charge over many things. Be happy because I'm really pleased with you!’
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: The one with two talents also came. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘You gave me two talents. Look, I've made a profit of two talents.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His master said to him, ‘You've done well—you are a good, faithful servant. You have proved trustworthy over small things, so now I'm placing you in charge over many things. Be happy because I'm really pleased with you!’
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: Then the man with one talent came. ‘Master,’ he said, ‘I know that you're a hard man. You reap where you didn't sow, and you harvest crops that you didn't plant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So since I was afraid of you I went and buried your talent in the ground. Look, you can have back what belongs to you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But his master answered him, ‘You wicked and lazy servant! If you think I reap where I don't sow, and harvest crops I didn't plant,
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: then you should have deposited my silver in the bank so that when I returned I could have had my money with interest.
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: Take the talent away from him, and give it to the one with ten talents.
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: For everyone who has will be given even more; and everyone who doesn't have anything, even what they have will be taken away from them.
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: Now throw this useless servant out into the darkness where there'll be crying and gnashing of teeth.’
segments.speaker: Master

segments.text: But when the Son of man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then he will sit on his majestic throne.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everyone will be brought before him. He will separate them from one another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He'll place the sheep on his right hand, and the goats on his left.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the king will say to the ones on his right, ‘Come, you who are blessed by my Father, and inherit the kingdom that's been prepared for you from the beginning of the world.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: For I was hungry, and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty, and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger, and you invited me in.
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: I was naked and you clothed me. I was sick and you looked after me. I was in prison and you visited me.’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: Then those who are right will answer, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry and fed you, or thirsty and gave you a drink?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When did we see you as a stranger and invite you in, or naked and clothe you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When did we see you sick, or in prison, and visit you?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The king will tell them, ‘I tell you the truth: whatever you did for one of these least important brothers of mine you did for me.’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: He'll also say to those on his left, ‘Go away from me, you who are doomed, into the eternal[^159] fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For I was hungry and you didn't give me anything to eat. I was thirsty and you didn't give me a drink.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I was a stranger and you didn't invite me in. I was naked and you didn't clothe me. I was sick and in prison and you didn't visit me.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then they will also answer, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and didn't look after you?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he will tell them, ‘I tell you the truth: whatever you didn't do for one of these least important brothers of mine you didn't do for me.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They will go away into eternal condemnation, but those who are good will enter eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus
